creator: Justin Welsh
pattern_note: Hooks are compact and mobile-first, with the first two lines doing most of the work before the fold.
reference_summary: Short opener, clean spacing, specific point of view, and simple framework language that feels immediately usable.

creator: Ross Simmonds
pattern_note: Educational posts tend to open with a strong opinion, then move fast into a tactical framework or distribution lesson.
reference_summary: Teach one useful thing, structure it cleanly, and make the post feel like a practical operator memo instead of a generic tip thread.

creator: Dave Gerhardt
pattern_note: Best posts usually feel like a direct category point of view from someone who has lived the problem.
reference_summary: Opinionated opening, conversational body, and a tight lesson that turns a market observation into a buying insight.

creator: Lara Acosta
pattern_note: High-performing posts are usually easy to scan, built around an immediate hook, and stripped of dead weight.
reference_summary: Visible white space, short lines, fast payoff, and a strong reader orientation make the post feel native to the feed.

creator: LinkedIn Marketing Solutions
pattern_note: Originality, authenticity, and useful perspective are core to thought leadership effectiveness on LinkedIn.
reference_summary: Posts should bring a distinct point of view and add insight on trends or customer challenges rather than recycle generic commentary.

creator: Shield
pattern_note: Performance loops should look at what actually drove reach and engagement, not just posting volume.
reference_summary: Treat previous post metrics as signal. Double down on hooks, formats, and topics that produced saves, comments, and distribution.
